concepts/order-and-replay.title: Order and Replay
concepts/order-and-replay.description: Signal treats replay and duplicate delivery as normal conditions.

concepts/versioning.title: Versioning
concepts/versioning.description: Versioning is part of every public Signal operation name.

examples/custom-transport-skeleton.title: Custom Transport Skeleton
examples/custom-transport-skeleton.description: See packages/examples/custom-transport-skeleton/ for an explicit dispatcher skeleton for future broker-backed adapters.

examples/http-post-publication-flow.title: HTTP Post Publication
examples/http-post-publication-flow.description: See packages/examples/http-post-publication/ for a thin HTTP binding over the same post publication runtime.

examples/minimal-runtime-flow.title: Minimal Runtime
examples/minimal-runtime-flow.description: See packages/examples/minimal-runtime/ for the smallest useful Signal runtime.

examples/storage-backed-idempotency-flow.title: Storage-Backed Idempotency
examples/storage-backed-idempotency-flow.description: See packages/examples/storage-backed-idempotency/ for the PostgreSQL idempotency-store variant of the post publication flow.

guides/emit-and-consume-events.title: Emit and Consume Events
guides/emit-and-consume-events.description: Use versioned event names and replay-safe subscribers.

guides/http-binding.title: HTTP Binding
guides/http-binding.description: The HTTP binding transports Signal messages without redefining the protocol.

guides/in-process-runtime.title: In-Process Runtime
guides/in-process-runtime.description: The in-process runtime is the simplest way to exercise the Signal contract.

introduction.title: Signal Protocol v1
introduction.description: Signal is a transport-independent application protocol and reference runtime for versioned queries, mutations, and events.

reference/capabilities.title: Capabilities
reference/capabilities.description: Capability documents describe the actual runtime surface.

reference/conformance.title: Conformance
reference/conformance.description: A Signal-compatible implementation preserves the public contract, not the internal Node.js implementation details.

reference/envelope.title: Envelope
reference/envelope.description: Every public Signal message uses the standard envelope.

reference/errors.title: Errors
reference/errors.description: Signal failures are structured and categorized.
